preamble: The shift from the alveolar stop [t] to the retroflex stop [ʈ] is a form of retroflexion. It typically occurs through sandhi assimilation, where an adjacent rhotic consonant pulls the articulation point backward from the alveolar ridge to the hard palate.

examples.note: Proto-Dravidian reconstructed a retroflex stop series distinct from dental. Tamil ட (ṭ) contrasts with த (t): 'aṭi' (foot) [ʈ] vs 'ati' (excessive) [t̪]. The retroflexion is lexically contrastive, not conditioned.
Recursive sandhi where /r/ + /t/ merges into a single voiceless retroflex stop.
The RUKI rule and subsequent retroflexion caused dental stops to shift into the retroflex position in clusters.
